summary: Executed backend + frontend validation for new warehouse layout module. Core API flows pass under normal ISO timestamps, but a critical mixed datetime format bug causes 500s in add/state/retrieve and blocks layout UI loading.

critical.issue: 500 Internal Server Error when layout data contains mixed offset-aware (sample data) and offset-naive (UI datetime-local) arrival_time values.

ui_bugs.issue: After submitting Add Container with datetime-local value, layout page gets stuck in loading because subsequent /api/layout/state returns 500.

integration_issues.flow: Configure -> Seed sample -> Add container with arrival datetime -> Retrieval
integration_issues.issue: Backend crashes on mixed datetime timezone awareness; UI then cannot complete retrieval flow.

action_items: Normalize arrival_time parsing to always timezone-aware (or always naive) before sorting in layout optimizer.
Add backend guard/validation for datetime-local input format from frontend.
Add regression test: sample data + manual datetime-local container must not produce 500 and retrieval should work.
After fix, retest full frontend flow including Staff role access/analytics restriction in UI navigation.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~984 lines (>700 guideline): should be split into focused modules for maintainability.
layout optimization sort key compares datetimes directly without timezone normalization, causing runtime TypeError under real UI input.
seed_data_creation: Used /api/layout/containers/sample with replace_existing=true and added temporary test containers (TEST-*, BUG-NAIVE-*).
context_for_next_testing_agent: If layout page is stuck in loading, reset layout data first. Repro bug reliably with: seed sample (aware timestamps) then add container with arrival_time like 2026-02-10T10:30 (naive); add/state/retrieve return 500.
rca of the issue: Root cause: parse_time() returns datetime.fromisoformat(value) as-is, so sample containers produce offset-aware timestamps while datetime-local UI input produces offset-naive timestamps. optimize_greedy sorts by parse_time(...) and Python raises TypeError for comparisons between aware and naive datetimes. Mitigation: normalize parsed datetime to UTC-aware consistently (or convert all to naive UTC) before sorting and persistence.
